title: eurya urophylla
intro: Eurya urophylla, also known as the Chinese rowan or umbrella tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. It is a tree native to China, Taiwan, and parts of Southeast Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eurya urophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
